name: Turmeric
summary: The golden spice whose active compound, curcumin, is among the most-studied natural anti-inflammatories — best known for easing joint pain.
superfoodReason: Its active compound curcumin has a large randomized-trial evidence base for reducing inflammation and arthritis symptoms.

benefits.claim: May ease arthritis and joint pain
benefits.mechanism: Curcumin, turmeric's yellow pigment, dampens inflammatory signalling pathways (such as NF-κB) in the joints. A meta-analysis of randomized trials found turmeric extracts and curcumin reduced the symptoms of arthritis, with effects comparable to some anti-inflammatory drugs in the pooled data.

citations.title: Efficacy of turmeric extracts and curcumin for alleviating the symptoms of joint arthritis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials (Daily et al., J Med Food 2016)

benefits.mechanism: Beyond the joints, curcumin reduces body-wide inflammation. A meta-analysis found curcuminoid supplements significantly lowered C-reactive protein (CRP), a blood marker of inflammation linked to many chronic diseases.

citations.title: Are curcuminoids effective C-reactive protein-lowering agents in clinical practice? Evidence from a meta-analysis (Sahebkar, Phytother Res 2014)

surprisingFacts.text: Curcumin is poorly absorbed on its own — but the piperine in black pepper can increase its absorption many-fold, which is why turmeric dishes and supplements almost always include pepper.
